title: Tax Knowledge Graph — ontology v1 (nodes + relationships)
description: IRD-grounded corpus → Neo4j. Step 10 adds ConsolidatedViewPassage + VIEW_* relationships for honest traceability from consolidated PDF text to underlying sections and amendment instruments.
design_rules: Every corpus chunk_id must link to at least one graph node (typically TextChunk).
Prefer manifest source_doc_id as the join key for LawInstrument.
Guides and draft instruments stay in the graph with lower authority_weight; OVERRIDES + edge dates express precedence (refined in Step 3c).
New rel types require ontology_version bump; do not overload one type with unrelated semantics.
ConsolidatedViewPassage nodes are optional: use them when ingesting a consolidated Act PDF so Proof Maps can jump to underlying Section / LawInstrument nodes, not only merged text.

node_labels.label: LawInstrument
node_labels.description: A versioned legal or interpretive source: Act, amendment, consolidated edition, circular, ruling, schedule publication, or official guide.

node_labels.label: Section
node_labels.description: A navigable unit inside an instrument: section, article, part, or schedule slice. Stable section_uid is assigned at ETL time (may combine instrument + section label).

node_labels.label: TextChunk
node_labels.description: Retrieval-sized text unit from corpus_v1; 1:1 with chunk_id. Bridge node for Proof Maps and vector stores.

node_labels.label: Concept
node_labels.description: Tax concept or defined term (e.g. resident, gross income, employment income). May be anchored by statute wording or curated.

corpus_alignment.note: concept_id is internal; links via DEFINES / MENTIONS

node_labels.label: Relief
node_labels.description: A deduction, credit, or exemption family (e.g. personal relief, donation relief).

corpus_alignment.note: Often extracted from Tier A sections and official tables

node_labels.label: RateBand
node_labels.description: Numeric band or threshold row (tax slabs, relief ceilings, PAYE/APIT table rows).

node_labels.label: ProcedureMilestone
node_labels.description: Filing, payment, or instalment milestone (due date, return type, e-Service step).

corpus_alignment.note: Tier B/C procedural guides + statute cross-links

node_labels.label: IrdHubSummary
node_labels.description: Structured capture of high-signal IRD Income Tax hub HTML summaries; always cross-linked to Tier A evidence where claimed.

corpus_alignment.note: From ird_extract_html / hub crawl; lower authority than statute

node_labels.label: ConsolidatedViewPassage
node_labels.description: A navigational anchor inside a consolidated edition PDF: ties merged wording to underlying statute/amendment sources without pretending the consolidated file is the sole authority.

corpus_alignment.note: Curated or semi-automated; chunk_id optional link to TextChunk from consolidated corpus; lineage via VIEW_TRACES_* edges

relationship_types.description: Section sits within one LawInstrument (statute, amendment publication, etc.).
Attach corpus TextChunk to Section and/or LawInstrument (every chunk_id reachable from the graph).
Section defines a Concept (legal definition or core statutory sense).
TextChunk mentions a Concept (weak/heuristic link; curated in ETL).
A LawInstrument (e.g. amendment Act) modifies the legal content of a Section node.
A newer LawInstrument fully supersedes an older one (instrument-level succession).

Lex specialis-style precedence: the source node takes priority over the target (typically more specific Section over more general Section). Use effective_* on nodes and optional edge properties in Step 3c.
IrdHubSummary cites a TextChunk as Tier A/B/C evidence for a summary claim.
RateBand applies to a Concept (e.g. income band) or Relief (ceiling row).
ProcedureMilestone is grounded in a Section (statutory or consolidated basis).
Section covers or authorizes a Relief (deduction/credit family).
ConsolidatedViewPassage belongs to the consolidated LawInstrument (same source_doc_id as the consolidated PDF).
Consolidated passage traces to an underlying Section node (base Act or amendment-redacted section).
Consolidated passage traces to an amendment or other LawInstrument that contributed the underlying wording.
